query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "citevqa: benchmarking evidence attribution for trustworthy document in" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on "speaker attribution" (identifying who spoke) in political discourse, which is a distinct construct from the user's question regarding "evidence attribution" (linking claims to source documents) in the context of VQA and linguistics. While both involve LLMs and text analysis, the core mechanism and task (speaker diarization vs. document-grounded evidence retrieval) do not satisfy the criteria for same-mechanism evidence, shared variables, or foundational methodology for the
